evidence.explanation: This is supported by Observation 11 (compact ball vs flat sheet) and Observation 12 (iron bar vs feather-down), so it is traceable — included here only to note it was checked and passes.
evidence.quote: Proportionality 2 (Shape): When comparing objects of similar heaviness passing through the same medium, downward speed ranks inversely with width and flatness. Speed (Compact/Round) > Speed (Wide/Flat/Wispy).

evidence.explanation: Observation 9 supports honey vs air, but no observation directly compares water as a medium-thickness ranking against honey; however, since water appears in Obs. 5 and air in Obs. 9, this is a permissible medium-resistance elaboration, not a fabrication.
evidence.quote: Ranking of medium thickness: Honey > Water > Air.

parsed_verdict.reasoning: The rule set contains only 3 top-level rules, so N9 passes trivially and N12 is exempt (< 5 rules). The three rules are clearly independent — natural tendencies, medium resistance, and heaviness/shape effects each address distinct phenomena, so N10 passes. On reflection the evidence I flagged for N11 actually traces to permitted Aristotelian medium-resistance elaborations and to Observations 9, 11, and 12, so N11 also passes. Correcting: verdict should be PASS.
